sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Those last 2 clicks were `no_impact` - they only changed the HUD band (rows 0-0), not the gameplay. So the bottom blue button can only shift so far right before hitting a limit. The green object is stuck at cols 20-21 while the gap is at cols 28-29.
This means I can't just shift the green object all the way to the gap using only the bottom blue button. I need a different approach.
Let me reconsider the puzzle. In Level 2, there are multiple blue buttons controlling different sections. Maybe I need to:
1. Shift the black bar (with the gap) to align with the green object, rather than shifting the green object to the gap
2. Or use different blue buttons to shift different sections independently
The black bar with the green gap is at rows 40-43. The green object is at rows 52-57. The bottom blue button (rows 44-47) shifts the bottom section. But what about the middle blue buttons?
Let me think about which blue button controls which section:
- Upper blue (rows 16-19): controls rows 1-19 (top white area)
- Middle blue (rows 24-27): controls rows 24-39 (middle white area)
- Lower blue (rows 36-39): could control rows 40-43 (black bar with gap)?
- Bottom blue (rows 44-47): controls rows 44-63 (bottom section with green object)
If the lower blue button controls the black bar section, I could shift the gap leftward to meet the green object!
Green object: cols 20-21
Gap: cols 28-29
Difference: 8 columns
If I shift the gap LEFT by 8 columns (2 shifts of 4), the gap would be at cols 20-21, aligning with the green object.
Let me try clicking the lower blue button (rows 36-39) to shift the gap section.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The bottom blue button hit its limit - green object stuck at cols 20-21
# Gap is at cols 28-29. Need to shift the GAP toward the object instead.
# Try the lower blue button (rows 36-39) which might control the black bar section
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 37, 'col': 2}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
